calendar.matters:
MOTION FOR WRIT OF MANDATE / Notice Of Hearing On Petitioners Writ Of Mandate From The Suspension Of His Driving Privilege
Off Calendar Mar-13-2018 Denied
MINI MINUTES: Appearances:
William T. Darden, Deputy Attorney General 510-879-0096 for Respondent State of California Dept. of Motor Vehicles.
No other Appearance.

RULING - The matter is Unopposed. The Court adopts the tentative ruling as follows:

PETITIONER TIMOTHY POWERS' MOTION FOR WRIT OF MANDATE From The Suspension Of His Driving Privilege.

Petitioner Timothy Powers' petition for writ of mandate is denied. The petition is both untimely and substantively without merit. Mr. Powers is seeking relief from an order that was served on April 28, 2017. Per Vehicle Code 13559, Mr. Powers had 30 days to seek judicial review of that order and an additional 4 days is added per Vehicle Code 23 due to mail service. Mr. Powers filed this case on June 9, 2017, which was 8 days late. Mr. Powers' citation to the 90-day period in Vehicle Code 14401 does not save his claim because the 30-day period of section 13559 applies rather than more general provision of section 14401. (Johanson v. Department of Motor Vehicles (1995) 36 Cal.App.4th 1209, 1215.) Sufficient evidence established that Mr. Powers was properly arrested for violating Vehicle Code 23152 and 40300.5. (DMV025.) The hearing before the DMV has relaxed evidentiary rules and the evidence showed that Mr. Powers was actually driving. (Lake v. Reed (1997) 16 Cal.4th 448, 461-462.) A witness reported that the vehicle had moved up the street, Mr. Powers was alone behind the wheel, he was 80 miles from his house, and he admitted to drinking the previous night. (DMV021-026.) The record demonstrates that Mr. Powers refused to submit to the breath and blood test. Mr. Powers did not complete the breath test and refused the blood test. (DMV025.) Mr. Powers' argument that he was so incapacitated that he did not understand the admonition fails. Cases have rejected this argument as it would illogically allow a more intoxicated person to have less accountability under the driving statues. (Bush v. Bright (1968) 264 Cal.App.2d 788, 792.)

Order is signed.(see scanned order for details)

Judge: Harold Kahn, Clerk: M. Goodman, Not Reported. (D302/HK)

MOTION FOR WRIT OF MANDATE / Notice Of Hearing On Petitioners Writ Of Mandate From The Suspension Of His Driving Privilege
Off Calendar Feb-14-2018 Continued To Mar-13-18 At 09:30 A.M. In Dept 302
MINI MINUTES: Appearances:
Francisco Rodriguez, Esq. 415-378-4133 for Petitioner Timothy Powers.
William Darden, Deputy Attorney General, 510-879-0096 of the Office of the Attorney General for Respondent Staet of California Dept.of Motor Vehicles.

RULING - The matter is discussed. Hearing is held. The Court informs the parties the hearing came on calendar at around 3:30 pm. The Court suggests for Petititioner to re-draft his petition to include citations. (as fully reported in the reporter's notes) The Court having reviewed the pleadings rules as follows:

PETITIONER TIMOTHY POWERS' Writ Of Mandate From The Suspension Of His Driving Privilege is continued to March 13, 2018 at 9:30 am. Petitioner's new moving papers are due on 2/20/18. Respondent's Opposition Papers are due by - 3/6/18. Reply due by - 3/8/18. (as fully reported in the reporter's transcript)

Judge: Harold Kahn, Clerk: M. Goodman, Court Reporter: Maria Torreano, CSR#8600, torreanom@aol.com, Reported. (D302/HK)
